title: Thinking Through the Thunder
description: Minimal pairs: THINK / TINK / FINK — TREE / THREE / FREE. Joca the programmer debugs while a storm rolls in, and pop songs give us the drills.
summary_pt_br: Pares mínimos que revelam as armadilhas de TH para brasileiros: THINK x TINK x FINK, THREE x TREE x FREE, BATH x BAT. Usando Thunder (Imagine Dragons) e Thunderstruck (AC/DC) como referência.

scenes.text_en:
Joca stared at his screen. A bug, a loop, a glitch.
'I THINK,' he said. 'I THINK. Not TINK. Not FINK. Which?'
A THUNDER crashed. The windows shook.
Joca smiled. 'A perfect /θ/ for a coder's book.'
scenes.text_pt:
Joca olhava pra tela. Bug, loop, glitch na mira.
'I THINK,' ele disse. 'I THINK. Não TINK. Não FINK. Lira?'
Um TROVÃO estourou. As janelas tremeram.
Joca sorriu. 'Um /θ/ perfeito pra quem programa.'

scenes.title: The three ways Brazilians get TH wrong
scenes.body_en: When you say /t/ instead of /θ/, 'think' becomes 'tink' (doesn't mean anything). When you say /f/, 'three' becomes 'free' (totally different word!). When you say /s/, 'thanks' becomes 'sanks' (not English). Only the tongue-between-teeth sound is correct.
scenes.body_pt: Três erros comuns: trocar /θ/ por /t/ ('tink' em vez de 'think'), por /f/ ('free' em vez de 'three', e aí vira OUTRA palavra), ou por /s/ ('sanks' em vez de 'thanks'). Só a língua entre os dentes é a forma certa.

examples.en: THINK (correct) vs TINK (wrong — no word) vs FINK (slang for snitch!)
examples.pt: THINK certo; TINK não existe; FINK é gíria pra dedo-duro
Confundir essas três muda o sentido totalmente

items.pt: três vs árvore — UM é ar entre os dentes, o outro é 't' seco

exercise.question: You hear a song say 'I can't stop ___ of you.' Which word fits?
exercise.explanation: THINKING /ˈθɪŋkɪŋ/ — 'I can't stop thinking of you' is one of the most common English song lines of all time.
exercise.hint_pt_br: THINKING com /θ/. 'I can't stop thinking of you' é uma das linhas mais famosas da música em inglês.

scenes.text_en:
Imagine Dragons sings: 'THUNDER! Feel the THUNDER!'
AC/DC roared: 'THUNDERSTRUCK!'
And Bia hums: 'I can't stop THINKING of the ocean'.
Three songs, one sound — the voiceless TH is the luck.
scenes.text_pt:
Imagine Dragons canta: 'THUNDER! Feel the THUNDER!' (Trovão! Sinta o trovão!)
AC/DC ruge: 'THUNDERSTRUCK!' (Atingido pelo trovão!)
E a Bia cantarola: 'I can't stop THINKING of the ocean' (não paro de pensar no oceano).
Três músicas, um só som — o /θ/ é a sorte, a devoção.

exercise.question: Complete the Imagine Dragons line: 'Feel the ___!' (loud storm noise)
exercise.explanation: THUNDER /ˈθʌn.dɚ/ — the /θ/ is crisp in this song. Play it loud and mimic.
exercise.hint_pt_br: THUNDER com /θ/. Coloca essa música bem alta e imita o som.

turns.en: TREE coffees? You want me to climb a tree?
turns.pt: TREE cafés? Quer que eu suba numa árvore?

turns.en: THREE. With my tongue between my teeth.
turns.pt: THREE. Com a língua entre os dentes.

turns.en: THREE it is. Thanks for the drill.

exercise.explanation: Five words in three minimal pairs. Mispronouncing /θ/ changes meaning — 'three' (3) is NOT 'tree' (árvore) is NOT 'free' (grátis).
exercise.hint_pt_br: Falar errado MUDA o sentido: three ≠ tree ≠ free. Treine os três bem separados.

exercise.question: 'I'm going to take a ___ after the beach.' (shower/bath)
exercise.explanation: BATH /bæθ/ ends with /θ/. 'Bat' is the animal/baseball-stick.
exercise.hint_pt_br: BATH termina com /θ/. 'Bat' é morcego ou taco de beisebol — outra palavra.

exercise.question: Complete: 'The ___ leads to the ocean.' (caminho, trail)

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Thinking Through the Thunder". Minimal pairs: THINK / TINK / FINK — TREE / THREE / FREE. Joca the programmer debugs while a storm rolls in, and pop songs give us the drills. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Thinking Through the Thunder". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Thinking Through the Thunder'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Thinking Through the Thunder".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.

sources.label: BBC Learning English · Beginners
sources.pt_hint: Curso gratuito da BBC, nível iniciante.

sources.label: VOA Learning English
sources.pt_hint: Notícias em inglês lento — ótimo para ouvido iniciante.
